items.question: Does your codebase contain any sensitive data directly (e.g. passwords, tokens, API keys, external URLs, json dumps, sql data etc)?
items.guidance: You should always use environment variables, github secrets and never expose such credentials to public repos

items.question: When deployed to some public domain, is it accessible by crawlers, bots and search engines, or the entire solution is protected at least with http-auth layer?
items.guidance: Consider if your tool should be publicly accessible or protected with authentication

items.question: Do you consolidate some sensitive data from various clients of ours into one place (ui, files, docs etc.) that if exposed can be a benefit to our competitors?
items.guidance: Assess if aggregated data could provide competitive advantage if exposed

items.question: Do you have sufficient documentation about your solution so some other colleagues, product or engineering, can contribute to your tool independently?
items.guidance: Documentation should enable independent contribution by team members

items.question: Do you expect the tool to become a part of our product portfolio at some point? Is it using Mantine as a primary UI library?
items.guidance: Consider alignment with product portfolio standards, including UI library consistency

items.question: Do you have some business logic rules in your solution that are transforming or filtering the data you use? It should be always maintained with a spec in a form of PRD document describing the feature logic
items.guidance: Business logic should be documented in PRD format for maintainability

items.question: Do you create some custom UI elements on top of default Mantine that needs some additional Storybook-like documentation?
items.guidance: Custom UI components should be documented similar to Storybook for reusability

items.question: Do you use Tailwind or any other Design System tokenising approach to build your UI?
items.guidance: Design system tokens ensure consistency and maintainability

items.question: Do you keep a hygiene of meaningful commit messages to your git repo, so anybody can understand the progress by looking at commit history?
items.guidance: Meaningful commit messages enable understanding of project evolution

items.question: Do you keep your code in a private repo?
items.guidance: Private repositories protect sensitive code and business logic
